answer:
Snowflake offers a free trial account, which provides full access to features for testing purposes without requiring payment information at signup [1]. You can register for this via a self-service form on the Snowflake website [1]. Converting a trial account to a paid, self-service account occurs when you add a payment method [1].

Developers can access Snowflake programmatically using the Snowflake REST API for resource management [2]. This API is compliant with the OpenAPI 3.1.0 specification, allowing for integration using most programming languages by submitting REST requests via tools like curl or Postman [2].
